cards.front: What is the general structure of a Unix command?
cards.back: Command-line interface: option, argument, and target file/directory

cards.front: What does the Unix pipe symbol (|) represent?
cards.back: Pipe: redirects the output of one command as the input for the next command

cards.front: What is the purpose of the Unix redirection operators?
cards.back: Redirect input/output to files or other commands

cards.front: What is the function of the Unix shell?
cards.back: Shell: interprets and executes commands, provides a command-line interface to the operating system

cards.front: What is the difference between a Unix option and a Unix argument?
cards.back: Option: a letter or word modifying the behavior of a command, Argument: a value or file/directory passed to a command
